channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: What to do when you hate your favorite influencers’ politics
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: Impact of Tariffs on Consumer Electronics and Streaming Services
item.description: Tariffs imposed by a second Trump administration could impact consumer electronics companies, streaming services like Netflix, and the overall economy.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: News influencers are overwhelmingly male. No wonder there’s a Republican bias
item.description: “News influencers” are social media translators, conveying the day’s biggest stories via TikTok commentary or Instagram graphic. They also happen to be overwhelmingly male. As trust in mainstream media lingers at an all-time low, many are turning to social media apps for their news.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Siete formas de implementar su propio LLM
item.description: La inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa es la nueva tecnología que más rápido se está desarrollando en la historia. Está transformando el mundo y, según la Encuesta de población en tiempo real a más de 3.000 adultos que trabajan en EE.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: We just got another hint that the Apple iPhone SE 4 is landing in March 2025
item.description: Previous rumors have pointed to a March 2025 launch for this phone, and now another analyst has weighed in.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: New trailer for The Studio looks like Apple TV Plus' star-studded cringe comedy response to HBO's superhero satire series The Franchise
item.description: The first trailer for The Studio, a new Apple comedy series starring Seth Rogen, will shine a tragicomedy-based light on the corporate world of movie making.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia's communications minister said Wednesday the government won't force social media users to hand over their personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Microsoft Copilot vs OpenAI ChatGPT: A Comparison of AI Chatbot Apps
item.description: Compare Microsoft Copilot and OpenAI ChatGPT, highlighting their differences and features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What is Rust? Safe, fast, and easy software development
item.description: A programming language can be fast, safe, or easy to write. As developers, we get to choose our priorities but we can only pick two. Programming languages that emphasize convenience and safety tend to be slow (like Python).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why the crypto community loves Howard Lutnick, Trump’s pick for Commerce secretary
item.description: Donald Trump’s selection on Tuesday of Howard Lutnick as Commerce secretary brought cheers from the cryptocurrency sector. Little surprise there. Lutnick has long advocated for cryptocurrency to be treated on equal footing with other financial assets.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Perplexity adds AI-assisted shopping features
item.description: Artificial intelligence search startup Perplexity on Monday launched a shopping hub to attract users, in a bid to beef up its platform as it attempts to take on Alphabet-owned Google’s dominance in the search engine space.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Astrophysicists Challenge Conventional View of Dark Energy
item.description: Dark energy, a mysterious force driving universe's expansion, may not be constant, challenging astrophysical models
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about Howard Lutnick, Trump's pick for commerce secretary
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has chosen Howard Lutnick, head of brokerage and investment bank Cantor Fitzgerald and cryptocurrency enthusiast, as his nominee for commerce secretary.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to “act on our behalf across our work and life.”.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Microsoft’s Windows 365 Link is a thin client device for shared workspaces
item.description: Microsoft will start selling a thin client device that lets workers boot directly to Windows 365 “in seconds,” the company announced on Tuesday. Windows 365 Link will cost $349 when it launches next April, but businesses can contact their Microsoft account team and request a private preview.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft and Atom Computing claim breakthrough in reliable quantum computing
item.description: Microsoft and Atom Computing have announced what they claim is a significant step forward in reliable quantum computing, unveiling a commercial quantum machine built with 24 entangled logical qubits.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to transform your architecture review board
item.description: I recall my angst about my first visit to an architecture review board (ARB), where a team of enterprise architects reviewed plans for new systems and application architectures.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: F# 9 adds nullable reference types
item.description: F# 9, the latest version of Microsoft’s open source functional language, has been released with support for nullable reference types and empty-bodied computation expressions. Standard library improvements also are featured, including random sampling functions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How ATO as code can reduce this compliance burden
item.description: OPM’s Dan Jacobs and stackArmor’s Gaurav “GP” Pal led an ACT-IAC team to develop a maturity model to help implement authority to operate as code. The post How ATO as code can reduce this compliance burden first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: The Onion CEO explains Infowars purchase amid Alex Jones's claims about auction
item.description: The Onion CEO Ben Collins confirmed that the publication purchased Alex Jones’s Infowars outlet in a Saturday series of posts on the social media platform Bluesky amid Jones's claims of wrongdoing and illegal activity.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk's companies balloon in value, with one exception
item.description: Tech giant Elon Musk has successfully expanded several businesses, bolstering his capital and financial prowess to become the richest person on Earth. But the billionaire entrepreneur has seen a sharp decline in the value of the social media platform X which he acquired in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: How to make your VPN faster
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how a VPN — virtual private network — works and how running it can slow down the operation of your device.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Exoskeleton helps paralyzed people regain independence
item.description: The WalkON Suit F1, an exoskeleton developed to help people with disabilities, can actually walk over to a person in a wheelchair, solving a major problem.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Strategies to navigate the pitfalls of cloud costs
item.description: In my line of work, I often hear CIOs and CFOs say, “Cloud providers are bleeding us dry.” As ever-increasing cloud bills roll in, they’re demanding answers about what they’re paying for and why they’re paying so much. Public cloud providers are eager to oppose this line of questioning.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: FBI, CISA say Chinese hackers breached multiple US telecom providers in targeted attack
item.description: The U.S. government said it is investigating after People's Republic of China hackers targeted commercial telecommunications service providers in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk says he and Trump have 'mandate to delete' regulations. Ethics laws could limit Musk role
item.description: In picking billionaire Elon Musk to be “our cost cutter” for the U.S. government, President-elect Donald Trump won't be the first American president to empower a business tycoon to look for ways to dramatically cut federal regulations. President Ronald Reagan tapped J.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KRON4
